message:
fix: 3 more unhashable-node paths follow-up to br-r37-c1-m0io3 (br-r37-c1-g438p)

Three remaining paths still mishandled unhashable inputs after the
br-r37-c1-m0io3 fix to ``add_node`` / ``add_edge`` / ``add_edges_from``:

(1) ``add_weighted_edges_from`` silently absorbed unhashable endpoints:
    >>> fnx.Graph().add_weighted_edges_from([([1,2], 3, 1.0)])
    # silently succeeded
    >>> nx.Graph().add_weighted_edges_from([([1,2], 3, 1.0)])
    TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

(2) ``remove_node`` raised the wrong exception class:
    >>> fnx.Graph([(1,2)]).remove_node([1,2])
    NetworkXError: The node [1, 2] is not in the graph.
    >>> nx.Graph([(1,2)]).remove_node([1,2])
    TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

The ``[1,2] not in self`` membership check silently returned False
on unhashable inputs, so remove_node fell through to the missing-
node error path instead of raising TypeError eagerly.

(3) ``Graph(...)`` constructor with edge-list containing unhashable
    endpoints:
    >>> fnx.Graph([([1,2], 3)])
    # nodes=[[1, 2], 3]; silently absorbed via Rust __new__
    >>> nx.Graph([([1,2], 3)])
    NetworkXError: Input is not a valid edge list

The Rust __new__ stores by Python id when nodes aren't hashable,
yielding a structurally-broken graph downstream.

Fix
---
``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``:

- ``_add_weighted_edges_from_with_attr``: hash-validate each
  endpoint up front, mirroring the ``add_edges_from`` fix from
  br-r37-c1-m0io3.

- ``_remove_node_with_networkx_missing_node_error``: call
  ``hash(n)`` before the membership check so unhashable inputs
  raise TypeError eagerly (matching nx).

- ``_init_absorbing_dict_of_dicts``: after ``raw_init`` (which
  returns whatever the Rust ``__new__`` already absorbed),
  iterate ``self.nodes()`` and call ``hash()`` on each — but ONLY
  for the passthrough path. The dict / numpy / pandas / Graph
  special cases below ``__init__`` already clear+rebuild from
  scratch, so any transient absorption of unhashable rows
  (e.g. numpy ndarray rows) doesn't need to flag here.
  On unhashable-detected, ``self.clear()`` and raise
  ``NetworkXError("Input is not a valid edge list")`` matching
  nx exactly.

Tests
-----
New ``tests/python/test_unhashable_followup_paths_parity.py``:
- test_add_weighted_edges_from_unhashable_endpoint_raises
  (parametrized over list / set / dict)
- test_add_weighted_edges_from_hashable_unchanged (regression)
- test_remove_node_unhashable_raises_type_error (parametrized)
- test_remove_node_missing_hashable_still_raises_networkxerror
  (the missing-but-hashable path is unchanged)
- test_constructor_edge_list_unhashable_endpoint_raises
  (parametrized over unhashable × all 4 graph classes = 12 cases)
- 5 special-case constructor regression guards (numpy / pandas /
  Graph / dict-of-list / edge-list with hashable nodes)

Full sweep
----------
6881 passed, 0 failed (was 6856 before — +25 new regression cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -71,6 +71,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fauol","title":"parity: ego_graph node ordering uses set iteration (random) instead of BFS-from-source order","description":"fnx.ego_graph node order is the iteration order of a set (arbitrary hash order). nx.ego_graph nodes are in BFS-from-source visit order (source first, then by distance from source). For node 'c' on graph with edges (c,d),(a,b),(b,c),(d,e), radius=2: fnx returns ['d','e','a','c','b'] but nx returns ['c','d','a','b','e']. Drop-in code that relies on iteration order breaks. Fix: iterate the BFS 'seen' dict (which records insertion order) instead of converting to set.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T11:07:07.265713125Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:10.734025927Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T11:10:10.732030928Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 76c3369. ego_graph now iterates nodes in G's insertion order (filtered to ego set) matching nx's G.subgraph contract. 10 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fs3bl","title":"min_weight_matching tuple direction drifts from nx","description":"fnx.min_weight_matching returns matched pairs with different tuple directions than nx (same family pattern as br-r37-c1-kpnc8 which fixed max_weight_matching).

Repro:
  edges = [(0,1),(0,2),(1,2),(2,3)]
  fnx -> {(0,1), (2,3)}
  nx  -> {(1,0), (3,2)}   <- tuple direction reversed

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx -> {('a','c'), ('d','e')}
  nx  -> {('e','d'), ('a','c')}

The matching choice itself is correct (same nodes paired); only the per-pair tuple direction differs. Drop-in code that compared the matching against a reference set of (u,v) pairs broke.

Fix: delegate min_weight_matching to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity, mirroring br-r37-c1-kpnc8 (max_weight_matching). Preserve the existing weight= and MultiGraph-projection support.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T17:25:25.186247477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T17:28:02.128625491Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T17:28:02.126228636Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-fuuor","title":"parity: random_labeled_rooted_forest stray q arg + tutte_polynomial positional x/y","description":"Two stale signatures: random_labeled_rooted_forest(n, q=None, seed=None) carried a stray q from random_unlabeled_rooted_forest (nx is just (n, *, seed=None)). tutte_polynomial(G, x=None, y=None) accepted x/y positionally as a numeric-evaluation extension; nx is (G,) only. Drop-in callers writing for nx and trying tutte_polynomial(G, 1, 1) had the call work on fnx but raise TypeError on nx. Aligned: rrf drops q (replaced by random_unlabeled_rooted_forest forwarder), tutte_polynomial makes x/y KEYWORD_ONLY. Also fixed tutte_polynomial recursion to use kwarg form internally. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T04:36:37.664864574Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:37:22.476756359Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:37:22.474915674Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4b6c6de. random_labeled_rooted_forest signature is now (n, *, seed=None) matching nx; tutte_polynomial keeps the x/y numeric-evaluation extension but as KEYWORD_ONLY so positional usage rejects identically. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-g438p","title":"follow-up: 3 more paths accept unhashable nodes (add_weighted_edges_from, remove_node, ctor)","description":"Follow-up to br-r37-c1-m0io3. Three remaining paths still accept unhashable types (or raise the wrong exception):

(1) add_weighted_edges_from with list endpoint:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph()
  >>> g.add_weighted_edges_from([([1,2], 3, 1.0)])
  # silently accepted
  >>> nx.Graph().add_weighted_edges_from([([1,2], 3, 1.0)])
  TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

(2) remove_node on unhashable node:
  >>> g = fnx.Graph([(1,2)])
  >>> g.remove_node([1,2])
  NetworkXError: The node [1, 2] is not in the graph.
  >>> nx.Graph([(1,2)]).remove_node([1,2])
  TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'

(3) Graph constructor with edge-list containing unhashable:
  >>> fnx.Graph([([1,2], 3)])
  # nodes = [[1,2], 3]; silently accepted
  >>> nx.Graph([([1,2], 3)])
  NetworkXError: Input is not a valid edge list

Drop-in code that catches TypeError on these paths won't trigger on fnx.

Fix:
- add_weighted_edges_from: hash-validate endpoints (mirrors the
  add_edges_from fix from br-r37-c1-m0io3).
- remove_node: hash-validate before the membership check.
- Graph(...) constructor: detect unhashable endpoints in the
  Rust __new__ override OR in the Python __init__ wrapper that
  already absorbs dict-of-dicts inputs.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T09:31:20.290571244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T09:31:53.691047647Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-g71v3","title":"find_cliques iteration order and clique node order drift from nx","description":"fnx.find_cliques iterates cliques in a different order from nx and within each clique returns nodes in canonical/sorted order rather than nx's pivot-based discovery order.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx -> [['a','b','c'], ['c','d'], ['d','e']]
  nx  -> [['e','d'], ['c','b','a'], ['c','d']]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,0),(2,3),(3,4),(4,2)]
  fnx -> [[0,1,2], [2,3,4]]
  nx  -> [[2,0,1], [2,3,4]]

Drop-in code iterating cliques in nx's order (or treating the first node of each clique as the pivot) breaks.

Fix: delegate find_cliques to nx for parity (mirrors articulation_points/bridges/cycle_basis fix pattern).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:32:48.946437257Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:39:24.990955650Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:39:24.988966752Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-g915s","title":"parity: greedy_color rejects callable strategy despite docstring claiming str or function","description":"fnx.greedy_color(G, strategy=callable) raises TypeError: argument strategy: function object is not an instance of str. The docstring claims str or function but the implementation only accepts strings (passes them straight to _raw_greedy_color). NetworkX accepts both str and callable. Fix: route callable strategies through nx via _call_networkx_for_parity, similar to interchange=True. Discovered while shipping br-75r94 (strategy_* wrappers).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:49:39.924775622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:09:39.089203090Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:09:39.087297771Z","close_reason":"Shipped in f02d0f6: greedy_color routes callable strategies through networkx (interchange=True path). Verified on C6 with string, fnx.strategy_*, nx.coloring.strategy_*, and lambda strategies — all produce identical colorings to nx.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-gyn8z","title":"parity: get_edge_data uses catch-all (u, v, *args, **kwargs) instead of nx's (u, v, default=None) / (u, v, key=None, default=None)","description":"fnx _private_aware_get_edge_data uses generic (self, u, v, *args, **kwargs) for both simple and multi graphs. nx exposes (u, v, default=None) on Graph/DiGraph and (u, v, key=None, default=None) on MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph. Drop-in introspection (IDE autocomplete, inspect.signature) returns the catch-all instead of the documented per-arg surface. Fix: split _private_aware_get_edge_data into _simple (u, v, default=None) and _multi (u, v, key=None, default=None) variants, mirroring the has_edge split done in br-r37-c1-lb47c. Add parity tests for both signature and behavioural kwarg-form calls (default= and key=).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T05:29:19.392821127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T05:31:25.356618305Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T05:31:25.354754878Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit b8cf88a. Split _private_aware_get_edge_data into _simple/_multi variants. inspect.signature returns nx's (u, v, default=None) on Graph/DiGraph and (u, v, key=None, default=None) on MG/MDG. 12 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -195,6 +195,12 @@ def remove_edge(self, u, v):
 
 def _remove_node_with_networkx_missing_node_error(remove_node_impl, *, graph_kind="graph"):
     def remove_node(self, n):
+        # br-r37-c1-g438p: nx raises TypeError('unhashable type: ...')
+        # before the membership check. fnx's ``n not in self`` ran the
+        # __contains__ path which silently returned False on unhashable
+        # nodes, causing remove_node to raise NetworkXError("not in
+        # graph") instead. hash() up front for nx parity.
+        hash(n)
         if n not in self:
             raise NetworkXError(f"The node {n} is not in the {graph_kind}.")
         return remove_node_impl(self, n)
@@ -1746,6 +1752,45 @@ def __init__(self, incoming_graph_data=None, multigraph_input=None, **attr):
         # attribute. Non-multigraph classes also accept it for nx
         # signature parity but ignore the value.
         raw_init(self)
+        # br-r37-c1-g438p: the Rust __new__ silently absorbs
+        # unhashable nodes (storing them by Python id) when the
+        # input is an iterable of edge tuples whose endpoints are
+        # unhashable types (list, set, dict). nx raises
+        # ``NetworkXError("Input is not a valid edge list")`` on
+        # those inputs. Validate that the just-absorbed nodes are
+        # hashable, but ONLY for the passthrough path — the
+        # dict / numpy / pandas / Graph special cases below clear
+        # and rebuild the graph from scratch, so any bad
+        # absorption from those is overwritten and doesn't need
+        # to be flagged at this point.
+        is_special_input = (
+            incoming_graph_data is None
+            or isinstance(incoming_graph_data, dict)
+            or (
+                type(incoming_graph_data).__module__.startswith("numpy")
+                and hasattr(incoming_graph_data, "shape")
+                and hasattr(incoming_graph_data, "ndim")
+            )
+            or (
+                type(incoming_graph_data).__module__.startswith("pandas")
+                and hasattr(incoming_graph_data, "iloc")
+                and hasattr(incoming_graph_data, "values")
+            )
+            or (
+                hasattr(incoming_graph_data, "nodes")
+                and hasattr(incoming_graph_data, "edges")
+                and callable(getattr(incoming_graph_data, "is_multigraph", None))
+            )
+        )
+        if not is_special_input:
+            try:
+                for node in list(self.nodes()):
+                    hash(node)
+            except TypeError as exc:
+                self.clear()
+                raise NetworkXError(
+                    "Input is not a valid edge list"
+                ) from exc
         if attr:
             self.graph.update(attr)
         if incoming_graph_data is None:
@@ -2055,10 +2100,20 @@ def add_weighted_edges_from(self, ebunch_to_add, weight="weight", **attr):
         to the weight. The fnx Rust method only takes (ebunch, weight),
         so fall back to per-edge add_edge when any trailing attrs are
         present. Parameter name matches nx (br-r37-c1-wcdm3).
+
+        br-r37-c1-g438p: hash-validate endpoints up front so unhashable
+        types (list, set, dict) raise nx-shaped TypeError instead of
+        being silently absorbed by the Rust binding (which keys nodes
+        by Python id when the value isn't hashable).
         """
+        materialized = list(ebunch_to_add)
+        for edge in materialized:
+            if isinstance(edge, tuple) and len(edge) >= 2:
+                hash(edge[0])
+                hash(edge[1])
         if not attr:
-            return raw(self, ebunch_to_add, weight=weight)
-        for edge in ebunch_to_add:
+            return raw(self, materialized, weight=weight)
+        for edge in materialized:
             if len(edge) == 3:
                 u, v, w = edge
                 self.add_edge(u, v, **{weight: w}, **attr)

@@ -0,0 +1,166 @@
+"""Parity for the 3 remaining unhashable-node paths after br-r37-c1-m0io3.
+
+Bead br-r37-c1-g438p (follow-up to br-r37-c1-m0io3). Three paths
+still accepted (or mis-classified) unhashable inputs after the
+initial fix:
+
+(1) ``add_weighted_edges_from``: silently accepted unhashable
+    endpoints; nx raises TypeError.
+
+(2) ``remove_node``: raised ``NetworkXError("not in graph")`` after
+    a silent-False membership check; nx raises TypeError before the
+    membership check.
+
+(3) ``Graph(...)`` constructor with edge-list containing unhashable
+    endpoints: silently absorbed via the Rust ``__new__`` (storing
+    by Python id); nx raises ``NetworkXError("Input is not a valid
+    edge list")``.
+
+The fix validates ``hash(node)`` on each endpoint at the wrapper
+edge: ``add_weighted_edges_from`` mirrors the ``add_edges_from``
+pattern, ``remove_node`` adds a hash check before membership,
+and ``__init__`` validates absorbed nodes for the passthrough path
+(skipping numpy/pandas/Graph special cases that clear+rebuild).
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import pytest
+
+import franken_networkx as fnx
+
+try:
+    import networkx as nx
+    HAS_NX = True
+except ImportError:
+    HAS_NX = False
+
+needs_nx = pytest.mark.skipif(not HAS_NX, reason="networkx not installed")
+
+UNHASHABLE = [
+    pytest.param([1, 2], id="list"),
+    pytest.param({1, 2}, id="set"),
+    pytest.param({"a": 1}, id="dict"),
+]
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# add_weighted_edges_from
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_add_weighted_edges_from_unhashable_endpoint_raises(val):
+    G = fnx.Graph()
+    GX = nx.Graph()
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        G.add_weighted_edges_from([(val, 3, 1.0)])
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        GX.add_weighted_edges_from([(val, 3, 1.0)])
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_add_weighted_edges_from_hashable_unchanged():
+    """Regression — hashable endpoints still produce the expected
+    weighted graph."""
+    edges = [(1, 2, 1.5), (3, 4, 2.0)]
+    G = fnx.Graph()
+    GX = nx.Graph()
+    G.add_weighted_edges_from(edges)
+    GX.add_weighted_edges_from(edges)
+    assert sorted(G.edges(data=True)) == sorted(GX.edges(data=True))
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# remove_node
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+def test_remove_node_unhashable_raises_type_error(val):
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        G.remove_node(val)
+    with pytest.raises(TypeError, match=r"unhashable type"):
+        GX.remove_node(val)
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_remove_node_missing_hashable_still_raises_networkxerror():
+    """A missing-but-hashable node still raises NetworkXError —
+    only unhashable inputs trigger TypeError."""
+    G = fnx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    GX = nx.Graph([(1, 2)])
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError):
+        G.remove_node(99)
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError):
+        GX.remove_node(99)
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Graph(...) constructor with edge-list containing unhashable
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("val", UNHASHABLE)
+@pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+def test_constructor_edge_list_unhashable_endpoint_raises(val, cls_name):
+    """nx raises ``NetworkXError("Input is not a valid edge list")``
+    when an edge-list contains an unhashable endpoint. fnx now
+    matches the exception class and message."""
+    cls = getattr(fnx, cls_name)
+    cls_n = getattr(nx, cls_name)
+    with pytest.raises(fnx.NetworkXError, match=r"Input is not a valid edge list"):
+        cls([(val, 3)])
+    with pytest.raises(nx.NetworkXError, match=r"Input is not a valid edge list"):
+        cls_n([(val, 3)])
+
+
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Special-case constructor inputs unaffected by the new hash check
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_constructor_numpy_adjacency_unchanged():
+    """Numpy adjacency input goes through a clear+rebuild branch,
+    so the new hash check must not fire on numpy ndarray rows
+    (which are themselves unhashable but only transiently absorbed
+    by the Rust __new__)."""
+    np = pytest.importorskip("numpy")
+    A = np.array([[0, 1, 0], [1, 0, 1], [0, 1, 0]])
+    G = fnx.Graph(A)
+    GX = nx.Graph(A)
+    assert sorted(G.edges()) == sorted(GX.edges())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_constructor_pandas_adjacency_unchanged():
+    pd = pytest.importorskip("pandas")
+    np = pytest.importorskip("numpy")
+    A = pd.DataFrame([[0, 1, 0], [1, 0, 1], [0, 1, 0]])
+    G = fnx.Graph(A)
+    GX = nx.Graph(A)
+    assert sorted(G.edges()) == sorted(GX.edges())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_constructor_from_graph_unchanged():
+    src = fnx.Graph([(1, 2), (2, 3)])
+    G = fnx.Graph(src)
+    assert sorted(G.edges()) == [(1, 2), (2, 3)]
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_constructor_dict_of_list_unchanged():
+    G = fnx.Graph({1: [2], 2: [3]})
+    GX = nx.Graph({1: [2], 2: [3]})
+    assert sorted(G.edges()) == sorted(GX.edges())
+
+
+@needs_nx
+def test_constructor_edge_list_hashable_unchanged():
+    edges = [(1, 2), ((3, 4), (5, 6))]
+    G = fnx.Graph(edges)
+    GX = nx.Graph(edges)
+    assert sorted(map(str, G.nodes())) == sorted(map(str, GX.nodes()))
